    # Local: z ∈ [−200, 200], sp ∈ [S_BASS_CLEAR, S_TREBLE_CLEAR] with the
    # y-axis flipped so treble (large sp) is at the TOP and bass (small sp)
    # at the BOTTOM — matches the physical orientation in the side view.
    # Content y range: [0, S_TREBLE_CLEAR − S_BASS_CLEAR] ≈ [0, 1726.45].
    out.extend(view_frame(sbf_x, sbf_y, sbf_w, sbf_h,
                          "Soundboard-face view (u, z)"))
    sbf_cx = sbf_x + 220   # origin for z=0
    sbf_cy = sbf_y         # local y=0 (= sp=S_TREBLE_CLEAR, treble end) at top
    out.append(f'<g transform="translate({sbf_cx},{sbf_cy})">')
    out.extend(sbf_view_content())
    out.append('</g>')

    # --- Front view ---
    out.extend(view_frame(front_x, front_y, front_w, front_h,
                          "Front view (yz, project along x)"))
    fr_cx = front_x + 220  # origin z=0
    fr_cy = front_y        # origin y=0
    out.append(f'<g transform="translate({fr_cx},{fr_cy})">')
    out.extend(front_view_content())
    out.append('</g>')

    # --- Top view ---
    out.extend(view_frame(top_x, top_y, top_w, top_h,
                          "Top view (xz, project along y)"))
    top_cx = top_x + 150       # offset for x; local x=0 to local_x=150
    top_cy = top_y + top_h/2   # z=0 in middle
    out.append(f'<g transform="translate({top_cx},{top_cy})">')
    out.extend(top_view_content())
    out.append('</g>')

    out.append('</svg>')

    with open(OUTPUT, "w") as fh:
        fh.write("\n".join(out))

    # Individual SVGs for standalone zoom in the HTML viewer.
    def standalone(elements, minx, miny, w, h, title):
        hdr = [
            f'<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>',
            f'<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" '
            f'viewBox="{minx} {miny} {w} {h}" '
            f'preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">',
            f'<title>{title}</title>',
            f'<rect x="{minx}" y="{miny}" width="{w}" height="{h}" fill="#fff"/>',
        ]
        return "\n".join(hdr + elements + ["</svg>"])

    # Side: x in [-60, 950], y in [0, 1940]
    with open(OUTPUT_SIDE, "w") as fh:
        fh.write(standalone(side_view_content(), -100, -20, 1100, 1980, "Side view"))
    # Top: x in [-150, 950], z in [-220, 220]; rotate -90° so treble ends
    # up at top and bass at bottom (matches side view orientation).
    # After rotate(-90): orig (x, z) → new (z, -x), so new x ∈ [-220, 220],
    # new y ∈ [-1000, 200].
    _top_elts = top_view_content()
    _top_wrapped = ['<g transform="rotate(-90)">'] + _top_elts + ['</g>']
    with open(OUTPUT_TOP, "w") as fh:
        fh.write(standalone(_top_wrapped, -240, -1020, 480, 1240, "Top view (rotated)"))
    # Front: z in [-220, 220], y in [0, 1940]
    with open(OUTPUT_FRONT, "w") as fh:
        fh.write(standalone(front_view_content(), -240, -20, 480, 1980, "Front view"))
    # Rear: same bounds as front (symmetric silhouette)
    with open(OUTPUT_REAR, "w") as fh:
        fh.write(standalone(rear_view_content(), -240, -20, 480, 1980, "Rear view"))
    # SBF: z in [-220, 220], sp in [-140, 1600]
    with open(OUTPUT_SBF, "w") as fh:
        fh.write(standalone(sbf_view_content(), -240, -20,
                            480 + 500, g.S_TREBLE_CLEAR - g.S_BASS_CLEAR + 40,
                            "Soundboard-face view"))

    print(f"wrote {OUTPUT}")
    print(f"also wrote: {OUTPUT_SIDE}, {OUTPUT_TOP}, {OUTPUT_FRONT}, {OUTPUT_REAR}, {OUTPUT_SBF}")
    print(f"canvas: {canvas_w:.0f} x {canvas_h:.0f} mm")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

     overlays everything. Click its "back" button or press Escape to return. */

    position: fixed;
    top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0;

<script src="svg-pan-zoom.min.js"></script>
<script>
  const panzoomInstances = {};

  async function loadView(el) {
    const src = el.dataset.src;
    try {
      const resp = await fetch(src);
      if (!resp.ok) throw new Error(resp.status + ' ' + resp.statusText);
      let text = await resp.text();
      // strip XML prolog for safe inline injection
      text = text.replace(/<\?xml[^?]*\?>/, '');
      el.innerHTML = text;
      const svg = el.querySelector('svg');
      if (!svg) return;
      // ensure it fills the container
      svg.removeAttribute('width');
      svg.removeAttribute('height');
      svg.setAttribute('width', '100%');
      svg.setAttribute('height', '100%');

      // Tighten the viewBox using svg.getBBox() on the root, which includes
      // child transforms. We hide the background <rect> while measuring so
      // it doesn't dilate the bbox.
      try {
        const bgRects = Array.from(svg.querySelectorAll('rect')).filter(
          r => (r.getAttribute('fill') || '').toLowerCase() === '#fff' &&
               r.parentNode === svg);
        bgRects.forEach(r => r.setAttribute('visibility', 'hidden'));
        const bb = svg.getBBox();
        bgRects.forEach(r => r.removeAttribute('visibility'));
        if (bb && bb.width > 0 && bb.height > 0) {
          const pad = Math.max(bb.width, bb.height) * 0.03;
          svg.setAttribute('viewBox',
            (bb.x - pad) + ' ' + (bb.y - pad) + ' ' +
            (bb.width + 2*pad) + ' ' + (bb.height + 2*pad));
        }
      } catch (e) { /* fall back to the file's viewBox */ }

      const pz = svgPanZoom(svg, {
        zoomEnabled: true,
        controlIconsEnabled: false,
        fit: true,
        center: true,
        minZoom: 0.2,
        maxZoom: 40,
        zoomScaleSensitivity: 0.3,
      });
      panzoomInstances[el.id] = pz;
    } catch (e) {
      const isFile = location.protocol === 'file:';
      const hint = isFile
        ? 'You opened this file directly (<code>file://</code>); browsers block fetch from disk. '
          + 'Run <code>python3 -m http.server 8001</code> from the project folder '
          + 'and open <code>http://localhost:8001/</code> instead.'
        : 'Served from <code>' + location.origin + '</code> but <code>' + src + '</code> was not found. '
          + 'Make sure the server is running in the <strong>Erand</strong> folder (on port 8001). '
          + 'Check: <code>curl -I ' + location.origin + '/' + src + '</code>.';
      el.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:1em;color:#a00;font-size:13px">'
        + 'Failed to load <code>' + src + '</code>: ' + e.message + '<br>' + hint + '</p>';
    }
  }

  document.querySelectorAll('.view-box').forEach(loadView);

  document.querySelectorAll('.reset-btn').forEach(btn => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const pz = panzoomInstances[btn.dataset.target];
      if (pz) { pz.resetZoom(); pz.center(); pz.fit(); }
    });
  });

  // Expand/collapse panel to fill the viewport.
  function toggleFullscreen(panel) {
    const entering = !panel.classList.contains('fullscreen');
    panel.classList.toggle('fullscreen');
    document.body.classList.toggle('has-fullscreen', entering);
    // Swap the button label between "expand" and "back".
    const btn = panel.querySelector('.expand-btn');
    if (btn) btn.textContent = entering ? 'back' : 'expand';
    // Re-fit the pan-zoom instance so the SVG rescales to the new panel size.
    const viewBox = panel.querySelector('.view-box');
    if (viewBox) {
      const pz = panzoomInstances[viewBox.id];
      if (pz) setTimeout(() => { pz.resize(); pz.fit(); pz.center(); }, 30);
    }
  }
  document.querySelectorAll('.expand-btn').forEach(btn => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const panel = btn.closest('.panel');
      if (panel) toggleFullscreen(panel);
    });
  });
  // Escape key exits fullscreen.
  document.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
    if (e.key === 'Escape') {
      const fs = document.querySelector('.panel.fullscreen');
      if (fs) toggleFullscreen(fs);
    }
  });
</script>

</body>
</html>

<!--
  clicky_side.svg - Clements 47 clicky-pen pitch mechanism.
  Rendered-CAD-style side elevation matching the reference render
  at clickypen.jpg.  1:1 mm scale.  Installed in the neck: barrel axis
  vertical, tri-lobed flange seated on the OUTER face of the plywood,
  shaft passes through the plywood into the string gap, paddle engages
  the string.

  Parts (top to bottom, colour-matched to the reference render):
    * green pusher            (#4CAF50)
    * translucent gray barrel (#d8d8d8, fill-opacity 0.6)
    * pink cam sleeve         (#f4b0a0) - visible through the barrel,
                                crenellated upper edge
    * blue tri-lobed flange   (#3f51b5) - three ears with screw holes
    * retaining ring          (small gear-like disc below the flange)
    * shaft + paddle          (inside the string gap, tangent to string)

  Dimensions kept to a few key numbers (OD 12, stroke 4, plywood 6,
  gap 12.7).  No section hatching.

  Barrel axis runs vertically, origin at the axis / top of the flange.
  +x : forward (toward the string)
  +y : DOWN (SVG)
-->

    <line x1="-48" y1="7.5"  x2="-5"   y2="7.5"/>

    <line x1="-48" y1="10.5" x2="-5"   y2="10.5"/>

    <line x1="-48" y1="29.6" x2="48"   y2="29.6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- ======================================================================
       SHAFT (extended state) + PADDLE
         Shaft Ø 6 mm, passes through the Ø 6.5 hole in the north sheet
         into the string gap.  Paddle at the shaft tip engages the string.
       ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Shaft, extended: from y = 6 (bottom of flange + retaining ring) down
       through the plywood (6..12) into the gap, tip at y = 22 -->
  <rect x="-3" y="6" width="6" height="16"
        fill="url(#gradShaft)" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.45"/>

  <!-- Paddle (trapezoidal) at the shaft tip, inside the gap.
       16 mm long along the string (edge-on in this view so we see its

       Drawn as a horizontal trapezoid tangent to the string. -->
  <path d="M -8,22 L -7,24 L 7,24 L 8,22 Z"
        fill="#d0c090" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <!-- Paddle thickness side -->
  <rect x="-8" y="22" width="16" height="0.5"
        fill="#a08060" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"/>

  <!-- Nose pin (small red-circle tangent stop at the string) -->
  <circle cx="0" cy="24.2" r="0.7" fill="#b0907a" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.2"/>

  <!-- String: small red circle, viewed end-on at the pitch point -->
  <circle cx="0" cy="25.0" r="0.55" fill="#c00000" stroke="#700000" stroke-width="0.15"/>

       ====================================================================== -->

    <!-- Shaft retracted (4 mm shorter into gap) -->

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!--
  dual_clicky.svg - Clements 47 dual clicky-pen assembly.
  Proposed for the treble strings (notably F7, G7; possibly D7, E7) where
  the standard single-clicky design fails: the nat and sharp pitch points
  on the string sit only 3-4 mm apart and two independent Ø 6.5 mm
  drilled holes collide.

  Solution - a dual-clicky assembly with a shared base:
    * One quad-lobed mounting flange (screw ears) hosts both mechanisms.
    * Two pushers side-by-side on top (green), each operating
      independently.  Barrels are spaced so the Ø 12 mm outer diameters
      clear each other (centres 16 mm apart).
    * Two cam sleeves inside their own barrels (pink), each with a
      crenellated top.
    * Inside the shared flange, the two shafts converge from the
      16 mm barrel spacing down to the 3.4 mm string spacing at the
      paddle tips via a short angled taper.
    * A single oblong drill slot (Ø 6.5 mm x 10 mm oval) in the plywood
      carries both shafts through into the string gap.
    * Two paddles tangent to a single red string circle, one at the nat
      pitch point, the other at the sharp pitch point 3.4 mm south.

  Rendered-CAD style matches pedal/clicky_side.svg.  1:1 mm.  Origin at
  the centre of the shared flange, +x toward the sharp (south) side of
  the string, +y DOWN (SVG).
-->
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     width="200mm" height="220mm"
     viewBox="-90 -115 200 220">

  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="gradGreen" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#2e7a33"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#5bbd5f"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#2e7a33"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradBarrel" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#b8b8b8"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#ececec"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#b8b8b8"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradPink" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#c88576"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#f4b0a0"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#c88576"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradFlange" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#283593"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#5c6bc0"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#283593"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradShaft" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#777"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#c5c5c5"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#777"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradPlyFace" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#b07a43"/>
      <stop offset="50%"  stop-color="#d49868"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#b07a43"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <style>
      .out   { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.6; fill:none; }
      .outf  { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.4; fill:none; }
      .thin  { stroke:#555; stroke-width:0.3; fill:none; }
      .ghost { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.45; fill:none;
               stroke-dasharray:1.4,1.0; opacity:0.75; }
      .cl    { stroke:#7070a0; stroke-width:0.25; fill:none;
               stroke-dasharray:3,1,0.3,1; }
      .lead  { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.25; fill:none; }

      .title { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:4.2px;
               fill:#111; font-weight:700; }
      .cap   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px; fill:#333; }
      .lbl   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.9px;
               fill:#111; font-weight:600; }
      .dim   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px; fill:#222; }
      .note  { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.3px; fill:#555; }
      .tag   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px;
               fill:#2e7a33; font-weight:700; }
    </style>

    <marker id="arr" viewBox="0 0 8 8" refX="7" refY="4"
            markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L8,4 L0,8 z" fill="#333"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

       TITLE / CAPTION

    Dual clicky pen - proposed for treble strings where nat/sharp pitch points collide
  </text>

  <!-- South plywood (solid - paddles stop above it) -->
  <rect x="-60" y="24.7" width="120" height="6"
        fill="url(#gradPlyFace)" stroke="#8b4513" stroke-width="0.5"
        opacity="0.85"/>

  <!-- Wood grain hints -->
  <g stroke="#9a6a3a" stroke-width="0.2" opacity="0.4">
    <line x1="-58" y1="7.5"  x2="-7"   y2="7.5"/>
    <line x1="-58" y1="9.0"  x2="-7"   y2="9.0"/>
    <line x1="-58" y1="10.5" x2="-7"   y2="10.5"/>
    <line x1="  7" y1="7.5"  x2="58"   y2="7.5"/>
    <line x1="  7" y1="9.0"  x2="58"   y2="9.0"/>
    <line x1="  7" y1="10.5" x2="58"   y2="10.5"/>
    <line x1="-58" y1="26.2" x2="58"   y2="26.2"/>
    <line x1="-58" y1="28.0" x2="58"   y2="28.0"/>
    <line x1="-58" y1="29.6" x2="58"   y2="29.6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- ======================================================================
       SHAFTS (extended state) + PADDLES
         Inside the flange (y 0 .. 6) each shaft converges from its
         barrel axis (x = -/+8) to the exit x at the base (x = -/+1.7)
         via a short angled taper.
         From the flange exit (y = 6) down through the shared drill slot
         (y 6 .. 12) and into the gap, the two shafts run vertically at
         x = -1.7 (nat) and x = +1.7 (sharp), 3.4 mm apart.
       ====================================================================== -->

  <!-- NAT shaft: converging taper inside flange (y 0 .. 6) -->
  <path d="M -11,0 L -5,0 L -0.7,6 L -2.7,6 Z"
        fill="url(#gradShaft)" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.45"/>
  <!-- NAT shaft: straight section from flange through plywood into gap -->
  <rect x="-2.7" y="6" width="2" height="16"
        fill="url(#gradShaft)" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.45"/>

  <!-- SHARP shaft: converging taper inside flange -->
  <path d="M 5,0 L 11,0 L 2.7,6 L 0.7,6 Z"
        fill="url(#gradShaft)" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.45"/>
  <!-- SHARP shaft: straight section -->
  <rect x="0.7" y="6" width="2" height="16"
        fill="url(#gradShaft)" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.45"/>

  <!-- NAT paddle (trapezoidal, tangent to string at nat pitch point) -->
  <!-- Paddle is 16 mm along the string (perpendicular to the page in
       real geometry, so we see it end-on as a narrower plate).  In the
       companion clicky_side view the paddle reads as a 16 mm horizontal
       span; here we are viewing across the string, so each paddle
       appears as a 6 mm x 2 mm face.  We still show the trapezoidal
       silhouette for style. -->
  <path d="M -5.0,22 L -4.2,24 L 0.8,24 L 1.6,22 Z"
        fill="#d0c090" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <rect x="-5.0" y="22" width="6.6" height="0.5"
        fill="#a08060" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"/>
  <!-- NAT nose pin -->
  <circle cx="-1.7" cy="24.2" r="0.7"
          fill="#b0907a" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.2"/>

  <!-- SHARP paddle -->
  <path d="M -1.6,22 L -0.8,24 L 4.2,24 L 5.0,22 Z"
        fill="#d0c090" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <rect x="-1.6" y="22" width="6.6" height="0.5"
        fill="#a08060" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"/>
  <!-- SHARP nose pin -->
  <circle cx="1.7" cy="24.2" r="0.7"
          fill="#b0907a" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.2"/>

  <!-- STRING: one red circle, viewed end-on at the pitch point.
       The string passes through both pitch points; here we draw it at
       the mid-point (x = 0) with the nat/sharp paddles tangent on
       either side.  In the physical harp the nat and sharp "points" are
       different points along the SAME string, 3.4 mm apart; in this
       end-on view we show both paddle noses touching the string. -->
  <circle cx="-1.7" cy="25.0" r="0.55"
          fill="#c00000" stroke="#700000" stroke-width="0.15"/>
  <circle cx="1.7" cy="25.0" r="0.55"
          fill="#c00000" stroke="#700000" stroke-width="0.15"/>
  <!-- Connecting line to indicate "same string, two pitch points" -->
  <line x1="-1.7" y1="25.0" x2="1.7" y2="25.0"
        stroke="#c00000" stroke-width="0.3" opacity="0.5"/>

  <!-- Spacing callout between paddle noses -->
  <line x1="-1.7" y1="27.5" x2="1.7" y2="27.5"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"
        marker-start="url(#arr)" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <text class="dim" text-anchor="middle" x="0" y="29.8">3.4 mm spacing</text>

  <!-- ======================================================================
       RETRACTED GHOSTS (dashed, 4 mm stroke each)
       ====================================================================== -->
  <g class="ghost">
    <!-- NAT shaft retracted -->
    <rect x="-2.7" y="6" width="2" height="12" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M -5.0,18 L -4.2,20 L 0.8,20 L 1.6,18 Z"/>
    <circle cx="-1.7" cy="20.2" r="0.7"/>
    <!-- SHARP shaft retracted -->
    <rect x="0.7" y="6" width="2" height="12" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M -1.6,18 L -0.8,20 L 4.2,20 L 5.0,18 Z"/>
    <circle cx="1.7" cy="20.2" r="0.7"/>
  </g>
  <text class="note" x="7" y="19.5">both retracted (3 mm air gap)</text>

  <!-- 4 mm stroke callout -->
  <line x1="-10" y1="20" x2="-10" y2="24"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"
        marker-start="url(#arr)" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <text class="dim" x="-18" y="23">stroke 4 mm</text>

  <!-- ======================================================================
       LABELS / LEADER LINES
       ====================================================================== -->

  <!-- Dual pushers title (single callout spanning both) -->
  <line class="lead" x1="13" y1="-80" x2="38" y2="-80"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="39" y="-79">dual pushers (nat | sharp)</text>
  <text class="note" x="39" y="-76.5">independent click per pitch; OD 10 mm each</text>

  <!-- Barrel label -->
  <line class="lead" x1="14" y1="-40" x2="38" y2="-40"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="39" y="-39">two translucent barrels, OD 12 mm each</text>
  <text class="note" x="39" y="-36.5">share a wall; 16 mm centre-to-centre</text>

  <!-- Cam sleeves label (left side) -->
  <line class="lead" x1="-12.2" y1="-48" x2="-38" y2="-48"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="-85" y="-49">two pink cam sleeves</text>
  <text class="note" x="-85" y="-46.5">each crenellated; click cycles</text>
  <text class="note" x="-85" y="-44">extend / retract per stack</text>

  <!-- Shared flange label -->
  <line class="lead" x1="-24" y1="2.5" x2="-38" y2="-4"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="-85" y="-5">shared flange (blue, 32 x 14 mm)</text>
  <text class="note" x="-85" y="-2.5">quad-lobed: 4 x M2.5 screws into plywood</text>

  <!-- Converging shafts label -->
  <line class="lead" x1="-6" y1="3" x2="-38" y2="10"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="-85" y="11">shafts converge inside flange</text>
  <text class="note" x="-85" y="13.5">16 mm -&gt; 3.4 mm over 6 mm thickness</text>

  <!-- Shared drill slot label -->
  <line class="lead" x1="-5" y1="9" x2="-38" y2="19"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="-85" y="20">shared drill slot (Ø 6.5 x 10 mm)</text>
  <text class="note" x="-85" y="22.5">single oblong carries both shafts</text>
  <text class="note" x="-85" y="25">vs. two colliding Ø 6.5 holes</text>

  <!-- Paddles label -->
  <line class="lead" x1="-5" y1="23" x2="-38" y2="34"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="-85" y="35">nat paddle | sharp paddle</text>
  <text class="note" x="-85" y="37.5">each tangent to string; nose r 0.8</text>

  <!-- Plywood labels -->
  <line class="lead" x1="-35" y1="9" x2="40" y2="-4"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="41" y="-3">6 mm plywood (north sheet)</text>
  <text class="note" x="41" y="-0.5">outer face carries shared flange</text>

  <line class="lead" x1="-35" y1="27.7" x2="40" y2="42"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="41" y="43">6 mm plywood (south sheet)</text>

  <!-- Gap callout -->
  <line x1="48" y1="12"   x2="48" y2="24.7"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"
        marker-start="url(#arr)" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <text class="dim" x="50" y="19">12.7 mm gap</text>

  <!-- Plywood thickness callout (north) -->
  <line x1="-65" y1="6"  x2="-65" y2="12"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"
        marker-start="url(#arr)" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <text class="dim" x="-76" y="9.5">6 mm</text>

  <!-- Overall flange length callout -->
  <line x1="-24" y1="-6" x2="24" y2="-6"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"
        marker-start="url(#arr)" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <text class="dim" text-anchor="middle" x="0" y="-7.5">flange OAL 48 mm (w/ ears)</text>

  <!-- Barrel OD callout on the left stack -->
  <line x1="-20" y1="-30" x2="-14" y2="-30"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"
        marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="-4" y1="-30" x2="-2" y2="-30"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.3"
        marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <text class="dim" x="-28" y="-31.5">OD 12</text>

  <!-- String label -->
  <line class="lead" x1="5.5" y1="25.0" x2="22" y2="40"/>
  <text class="lbl"  x="23" y="41">string (1 mm dia, end-on)</text>
  <text class="note" x="23" y="43.5">SAME string, two pitch points</text>
  <text class="note" x="23" y="46">(nat / sharp) 3.4 mm apart at G7</text>

  <!-- Footprint reassurance note -->
  <text class="note" x="-88" y="56">
    Footprint: 48 mm along string (incl. screw ears) x 14 mm across.
  </text>
  <text class="note" x="-88" y="59">
    Fits within the 17 mm treble string pitch in the cross-string
    direction; along-string length uses unused space between adjacent strings.
  </text>

</svg>

<!--
  tuner_side.svg - Clements 47 guitar-tuner, side elevation (product view).
  1:1 scale, millimeters.  Adapted from Thingiverse 6099101 by noamtsvi
  (CC-BY-NC).  Goal of this drawing: visually recognizable silhouettes of
  the real printed parts (teardrop worm-driver head + threaded shaft,
  rounded-rectangle case, thin cap, through gear post), in situ between
  two 6 mm plywood sides separated by a 12.7 mm string gap.
-->
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     width="180mm" height="160mm"
     viewBox="-80 -60 180 160">

  <defs>
    <style>
      .outl     { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.6;  fill:none; }
      .outlthin { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.35; fill:none; }
      .ply      { fill:#c79568; stroke:#8b4513; stroke-width:0.6; }
      .tuner    { fill:#3a6fd4; fill-opacity:0.55; stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.6; }
      .tuner2   { fill:#3a6fd4; fill-opacity:0.40; stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.5; }
      .cap      { fill:#5a8bdc; fill-opacity:0.55; stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.5; }
      .thread   { stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.5; fill:none; stroke-linecap:round; }
      .cross    { fill:#1d3f82; stroke:none; }
      .screw    { fill:#eaeaea; stroke:#555; stroke-width:0.25; }
      .strng    { fill:#c00000; stroke:#8a0000; stroke-width:0.25; }
      .cl       { stroke:#9090b0; stroke-width:0.2; fill:none;
                  stroke-dasharray:3,1,0.4,1; }

      .lbl      { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:3.0px; fill:#111;
                  font-weight:600; }
      .lblsm    { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.4px; fill:#333; }
      .note     { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.2px; fill:#555; }
      .attr     { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px; fill:#555;
                  font-style:italic; }
      .title    { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:4.0px; fill:#111;
                  font-weight:700; }
      .callout  { stroke:#555; stroke-width:0.25; fill:none; }
    </style>
  </defs>

  <!-- ================================================================
       Attribution caption (top-left) + title
       ================================================================ -->
  <text class="attr"  x="-78" y="-55">Guitar tuner (Thingiverse 6099101 noamtsvi, CC-BY-NC)</text>
  <text class="title" x="-78" y="-50">Clements 47 - guitar tuner, side view (1:1 mm)</text>
  <text class="note"  x="-78" y="-46">Worm-and-wheel direct drive. Parts shown as product silhouettes.</text>

  <!-- ================================================================
       Coordinate frame
         x = 0  : outer face of the north plywood (where the case sits)
         x grows to the right (into the string gap and beyond)
         y grows down (along the string direction, as in clicky_side.svg)
         north plywood : x = 0  .. 6
         string gap    : x = 6  .. 18.7   (12.7 mm)
         south plywood : x = 18.7 .. 24.7 (6 mm)
         case lives on the -x side of x=0.
         worm axis is VERTICAL (y), offset to the -x side of the gear post.
       ================================================================ -->

  <!-- PLYWOOD (background) -->
  <!-- North plywood, with a 16 mm clearance hole (y = -8 .. 8) for the post -->
  <path class="ply"
        d="M 0,-42 L 6,-42 L 6,-8 L 0,-8 Z"/>
  <path class="ply"
        d="M 0, 8 L 6, 8 L 6, 42 L 0, 42 Z"/>
  <text class="lblsm" x="2" y="-34" transform="rotate(-90 2 -34)">plywood (north, 6 mm)</text>

  <!-- South plywood (across the 12.7 mm string gap) -->
  <path class="ply"
        d="M 18.7,-42 L 24.7,-42 L 24.7,42 L 18.7,42 Z"/>
  <text class="lblsm" x="20.7" y="-34" transform="rotate(-90 20.7 -34)">plywood (south, 6 mm)</text>

  <!-- Gap dim hint -->
  <text class="note" x="7" y="40">12.7 mm gap</text>

  <!-- GEAR POST (through plywood, into gap) -->
  <!--
    Post is Ø 15.4 x 42.6 mm long, axis horizontal (runs along +x through
    the north ply and into the gap).  Seen edge-on in this view it reads
    as a horizontal capsule.  Starts inside the case near x = -17, ends
    inside the gap near x = +23.
  -->
  <rect class="tuner" x="-17.4" y="-7.7" width="42.6" height="15.4" rx="1.2" ry="1.2"/>
  <!-- little bevel ring where it meets the plywood (visual only) -->
  <line class="outlthin" x1="0"  y1="-7.7" x2="0"  y2="7.7"/>
  <line class="outlthin" x1="6"  y1="-7.7" x2="6"  y2="7.7"/>
  <line class="cl" x1="-25" y1="0" x2="30" y2="0"/>

  <!-- String wrapping around the post inside the gap.  Small red circle
       tangent to the post on the gap side. -->
  <circle class="strng" cx="12.4" cy="7.7" r="0.55"/>
  <text class="lblsm" x="14.2" y="9.4">string</text>

  <!-- ================================================================
       CASE - rounded rectangle on the outer face of the north plywood.
       Footprint in this view: 39.2 (y, along string) x 19.4 (x, out).
       We centre it so the worm axis (x = -9.7) lies near the middle.
       Case x: -19.4 .. 0.  Case y: -10 .. 29.2.
       ================================================================ -->
  <rect class="tuner" x="-19.4" y="-10" width="19.4" height="39.2" rx="2.8" ry="2.8"/>

  <!-- Cutout hint where the worm enters the case (small arc on the bottom,
       where the knob protrudes).  The worm exits the case on the -y side,
       i.e. at the top.  But the real tuner has the knob hanging out the
       far end of the case (along the worm axis).  We show a small
       rectangular cutout on the -x face where the shaft / cap meet. -->
  <rect x="-19.4" y="-3" width="0.5" height="23.7" fill="#1d3f82" fill-opacity="0.8" stroke="none"/>

  <!-- Small arch on the inner (+x) face where the gear post exits: -->
  <path class="outlthin"
        d="M 0,-8 A 2 2 0 0 1 0,8"/>

  <!-- ================================================================
       CAP - thin retaining plate on the outer (-x) face of the case.
       Footprint: 23.7 (y) x 4.3 (x).  Two small screws.
       ================================================================ -->
  <rect class="cap" x="-23.7" y="-3" width="4.3" height="23.7" rx="0.8" ry="0.8"/>
  <!-- Screws (two) on the cap -->
  <circle class="screw" cx="-21.55" cy="-0.5" r="0.9"/>
  <circle class="screw" cx="-21.55" cy="18.2" r="0.9"/>
  <!-- Tiny Phillips marks on screws -->
  <path class="outlthin" d="M -22.2,-0.5 L -20.9,-0.5 M -21.55,-1.15 L -21.55,0.15"/>
  <path class="outlthin" d="M -22.2,18.2 L -20.9,18.2 M -21.55,17.55 L -21.55,18.85"/>

  <!-- ================================================================
       WORM DRIVER - the teardrop-headed part from guitartuner.png.
       Oriented with the axis vertical (along +y), head at the top,
       threaded shaft below the head.
       We draw the ACTUAL PART OUTSIDE the case (to the south / below the
       case), as a free-standing product-view silhouette, because the
       worm-driver geometry is the highlight of this drawing.  A small
       ghost version also sits inside the case to show where it lives.

       Free-standing silhouette, centred at x = 40 (right of the assembly).
       Teardrop head: ~19 wide x 25 tall.  Neck: short transition, ~6 mm.
       Threaded shaft: ~10 wide x 25 tall, 4-5 thread coils.
       Total length ~47.7 mm.
       ================================================================ -->
  <g transform="translate(55, -30)">
    <!-- Teardrop head.
         Constructed as two cubic curves joining:
           top-centre (0, 0) - the rounded top
           sides (+/- 9.5, 13) - widest point slightly below middle
           bottom-centre (0, 25) - tapers into the neck
    -->
    <path class="tuner"
          d="M 0,0
             C -6,0 -9.6,5 -9.6,13
             C -9.6,20 -6,23 -3.2,24.5
             C -2,25 -1,25.5 0,25.5
             C  1,25.5  2,25  3.2,24.5
             C  6,23  9.6,20  9.6,13
             C  9.6,5  6,0 0,0 Z"/>
    <!-- Phillips cross on face of head (centred at (0, 12)) -->
    <rect class="cross" x="-3.8" y="11.6" width="7.6" height="0.9"/>
    <rect class="cross" x="-0.45" y="8" width="0.9" height="8"/>

    <!-- Small highlight arc on head (visual depth cue) -->
    <path class="outlthin" stroke-opacity="0.45"
          d="M -7.5,6 C -7,3 -4,1 -1,1"/>

    <!-- Neck between head and threaded shaft -->
    <!-- Head ends at y=25.5; shaft starts at y=31.5.  Neck is Ø ~6 mm. -->
    <path class="tuner"
          d="M -3.2,24.5
             C -3.2,27 -3,29 -3,31.5
             L 3,31.5
             C 3,29 3.2,27 3.2,24.5 Z"/>

    <!-- Threaded shaft.
         OD ~10 mm, axis vertical, from y=31.5 to y=47.7 (16 mm visible).
         Rendered as a stack of four thread "donuts" / slanted ovals so
         it looks like a screw thread, matching the photo.
    -->
    <!-- Cylinder backdrop (outline of the shaft as a whole) -->
    <rect class="tuner2" x="-5" y="31.5" width="10" height="16" rx="1.5" ry="1.5"/>

    <!-- Thread coils: 4 slanted ellipses suggesting a single-start thread -->
    <g>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="33.5" rx="5.0" ry="1.3" class="tuner"
               transform="rotate(-6 0 33.5)"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="37.0" rx="5.0" ry="1.3" class="tuner"
               transform="rotate(-6 0 37.0)"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="40.5" rx="5.0" ry="1.3" class="tuner"
               transform="rotate(-6 0 40.5)"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="44.0" rx="5.0" ry="1.3" class="tuner"
               transform="rotate(-6 0 44.0)"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="47.3" rx="4.6" ry="1.2" class="tuner"
               transform="rotate(-6 0 47.3)"/>
    </g>

    <!-- Slanted hash lines on each coil to give thread texture -->
    <g class="thread">
      <line x1="-4" y1="32.8" x2="4" y2="34.2"/>
      <line x1="-4" y1="36.3" x2="4" y2="37.7"/>
      <line x1="-4" y1="39.8" x2="4" y2="41.2"/>
      <line x1="-4" y1="43.3" x2="4" y2="44.7"/>
    </g>

    <!-- Length callout on the free-standing worm -->
    <line x1="11" y1="0"    x2="14" y2="0"    stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2"/>
    <line x1="11" y1="47.7" x2="14" y2="47.7" stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2"/>
    <line x1="13" y1="0"    x2="13" y2="47.7" stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2"/>
    <text class="lblsm" x="14.5" y="26">47.7 mm</text>
    <text class="lblsm" x="14.5" y="29">(total)</text>

    <!-- Head OD callout -->
    <line x1="-9.6" y1="-3" x2="-9.6" y2="-6" stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2"/>
    <line x1=" 9.6" y1="-3" x2=" 9.6" y2="-6" stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2"/>
    <line x1="-9.6" y1="-5" x2=" 9.6" y2="-5" stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2"/>
    <text class="lblsm" x="-4" y="-6.5">Ø 19</text>

    <!-- Shaft OD callout -->
    <line x1="5"  y1="38" x2="8"  y2="38" stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2"/>
    <line x1="-5" y1="38" x2="8"  y2="38" stroke="#555" stroke-width="0.2" stroke-opacity="0"/>
    <text class="lblsm" x="7" y="40">Ø 10</text>

    <!-- Caption under the free-standing worm -->
    <text class="lbl" x="-11" y="55">worm driver</text>
    <text class="note" x="-11" y="58">(teardrop head + threaded shaft)</text>
  </g>

  <!-- Ghost worm inside the case (shows the in-situ position) -->
  <!-- Worm axis is at x = -9.7, vertical.  Threaded portion of worm lives
       inside the case, roughly y = -2 .. 29.2 (31.2 mm). -->
  <g opacity="0.55">
    <!-- Threaded body (as a thin vertical capsule with coil hints) -->
    <rect x="-14.7" y="-2" width="10" height="31.2" rx="1.5" ry="1.5"
          class="tuner2"/>
    <g class="thread" stroke-opacity="0.7">
      <line x1="-13.7" y1="0.5" x2="-5.7" y2="1.5"/>
      <line x1="-13.7" y1="4.5" x2="-5.7" y2="5.5"/>

<script src="svg-pan-zoom.min.js"></script>
<script>
  const panzoomInstances = {};

  async function loadView(el) {
    const src = el.dataset.src;
    try {
      const resp = await fetch(src);
      if (!resp.ok) throw new Error(resp.status + ' ' + resp.statusText);
      let text = await resp.text();
      // strip XML prolog for safe inline injection
      text = text.replace(/<\?xml[^?]*\?>/, '');
      el.innerHTML = text;
      const svg = el.querySelector('svg');
      if (!svg) return;
      // ensure it fills the container
      svg.removeAttribute('width');
      svg.removeAttribute('height');
      svg.setAttribute('width', '100%');
      svg.setAttribute('height', '100%');

      // Tighten the viewBox using svg.getBBox() on the root, which includes
      // child transforms. We hide the background <rect> while measuring so
      // it doesn't dilate the bbox.
      try {
        const bgRects = Array.from(svg.querySelectorAll('rect')).filter(
          r => (r.getAttribute('fill') || '').toLowerCase() === '#fff' &&
               r.parentNode === svg);
        bgRects.forEach(r => r.setAttribute('visibility', 'hidden'));
        const bb = svg.getBBox();
        bgRects.forEach(r => r.removeAttribute('visibility'));
        if (bb && bb.width > 0 && bb.height > 0) {
          const pad = Math.max(bb.width, bb.height) * 0.03;
          svg.setAttribute('viewBox',
            (bb.x - pad) + ' ' + (bb.y - pad) + ' ' +
            (bb.width + 2*pad) + ' ' + (bb.height + 2*pad));
        }
      } catch (e) { /* fall back to the file's viewBox */ }

      const pz = svgPanZoom(svg, {
        zoomEnabled: true,
        controlIconsEnabled: false,
        fit: true,
        center: true,
        minZoom: 0.2,
        maxZoom: 40,
        zoomScaleSensitivity: 0.3,
      });
      panzoomInstances[el.id] = pz;
    } catch (e) {
      const isFile = location.protocol === 'file:';
      const hint = isFile
        ? 'You opened this file directly (<code>file://</code>); browsers block fetch from disk. '
          + 'Run <code>python3 -m http.server 8001</code> from the project folder '
          + 'and open <code>http://localhost:8001/</code> instead.'
        : 'Served from <code>' + location.origin + '</code> but <code>' + src + '</code> was not found. '
          + 'Make sure the server is running in the <strong>Erand</strong> folder (on port 8001). '
          + 'Check: <code>curl -I ' + location.origin + '/' + src + '</code>.';
      el.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:1em;color:#a00;font-size:13px">'
        + 'Failed to load <code>' + src + '</code>: ' + e.message + '<br>' + hint + '</p>';
    }
  }

  document.querySelectorAll('.view-box').forEach(loadView);

  document.querySelectorAll('.reset-btn').forEach(btn => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const pz = panzoomInstances[btn.dataset.target];
      if (pz) { pz.resetZoom(); pz.center(); pz.fit(); }
    });
  });

  // Expand/collapse panel to fill the viewport.
  function toggleFullscreen(panel) {
    const entering = !panel.classList.contains('fullscreen');
    panel.classList.toggle('fullscreen');
    document.body.classList.toggle('has-fullscreen', entering);
    // Swap the button label between "expand" and "back".
    const btn = panel.querySelector('.expand-btn');
    if (btn) btn.textContent = entering ? 'back' : 'expand';
    // Re-fit the pan-zoom instance so the SVG rescales to the new panel size.
    const viewBox = panel.querySelector('.view-box');
    if (viewBox) {
      const pz = panzoomInstances[viewBox.id];
      if (pz) setTimeout(() => { pz.resize(); pz.fit(); pz.center(); }, 30);
    }
  }
  document.querySelectorAll('.expand-btn').forEach(btn => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const panel = btn.closest('.panel');
      if (panel) toggleFullscreen(panel);
    });
  });
  // Escape key exits fullscreen.
  document.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
    if (e.key === 'Escape') {
      const fs = document.querySelector('.panel.fullscreen');
      if (fs) toggleFullscreen(fs);
    }
  });
</script>

</body>
</html>

<!--
  clicky_side.svg - Clements 47 clicky-pen pitch mechanism.
  Rendered-CAD-style side elevation matching the reference render
  at clickypen.jpg.  1:1 mm scale.  Installed in the neck: barrel axis
  vertical, tri-lobed flange seated on the OUTER face of the plywood,
  shaft passes through the plywood into the string gap, paddle engages
  the string.

  Parts (top to bottom, colour-matched to the reference render):
    * green pusher            (#4CAF50)
    * translucent gray barrel (#d8d8d8, fill-opacity 0.6)
    * pink cam sleeve         (#f4b0a0) - visible through the barrel,
                                crenellated upper edge
    * blue tri-lobed flange   (#3f51b5) - three ears with screw holes
    * retaining ring          (small gear-like disc below the flange)
    * shaft + paddle          (inside the string gap, tangent to string)

  Dimensions kept to a few key numbers (OD 12, stroke 4, plywood 6,
  gap 12.7).  No section hatching.

  Barrel axis runs vertically, origin at the axis / top of the flange.
  +x : forward (toward the string)
  +y : DOWN (SVG)
-->

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!--
  dual_clicky.svg - Clements 47 dual clicky-pen assembly.
  Proposed for the treble strings (notably F7, G7; possibly D7, E7) where
  the standard single-clicky design fails: the nat and sharp pitch points
  on the string sit only 3-4 mm apart and two independent Ø 6.5 mm
  drilled holes collide.

  Solution - a dual-clicky assembly with a shared base:
    * One quad-lobed mounting flange (screw ears) hosts both mechanisms.
    * Two pushers side-by-side on top (green), each operating
      independently.  Barrels are spaced so the Ø 12 mm outer diameters
      clear each other (centres 16 mm apart).
    * Two cam sleeves inside their own barrels (pink), each with a
      crenellated top.
    * Inside the shared flange, the two shafts converge from the
      16 mm barrel spacing down to the 3.4 mm string spacing at the
      paddle tips via a short angled taper.
    * A single oblong drill slot (Ø 6.5 mm x 10 mm oval) in the plywood
      carries both shafts through into the string gap.
    * Two paddles tangent to a single red string circle, one at the nat
      pitch point, the other at the sharp pitch point 3.4 mm south.

  Rendered-CAD style matches pedal/clicky_side.svg.  1:1 mm.  Origin at
  the centre of the shared flange, +x toward the sharp (south) side of
  the string, +y DOWN (SVG).
-->
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     width="200mm" height="220mm"
     viewBox="-90 -115 200 220">

  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="gradGreen" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#2e7a33"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#5bbd5f"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#2e7a33"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradBarrel" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#b8b8b8"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#ececec"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#b8b8b8"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradPink" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#c88576"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#f4b0a0"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#c88576"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradFlange" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#283593"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#5c6bc0"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#283593"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradShaft" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#777"/>
      <stop offset="45%"  stop-color="#c5c5c5"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#777"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <linearGradient id="gradPlyFace" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#b07a43"/>
      <stop offset="50%"  stop-color="#d49868"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#b07a43"/>
    </linearGradient>

    <style>
      .out   { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.6; fill:none; }
      .outf  { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.4; fill:none; }
      .thin  { stroke:#555; stroke-width:0.3; fill:none; }
      .ghost { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.45; fill:none;
               stroke-dasharray:1.4,1.0; opacity:0.75; }
      .cl    { stroke:#7070a0; stroke-width:0.25; fill:none;
               stroke-dasharray:3,1,0.3,1; }
      .lead  { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.25; fill:none; }

      .title { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:4.2px;
               fill:#111; font-weight:700; }
      .cap   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px; fill:#333; }
      .lbl   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.9px;
               fill:#111; font-weight:600; }
      .dim   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px; fill:#222; }
      .note  { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.3px; fill:#555; }
      .tag   { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px;
               fill:#2e7a33; font-weight:700; }
    </style>

    <marker id="arr" viewBox="0 0 8 8" refX="7" refY="4"
            markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L8,4 L0,8 z" fill="#333"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- ======================================================================
       TITLE / CAPTION
       ====================================================================== -->
  <text class="title" x="-88" y="-108">
    Dual clicky pen - proposed for treble strings where nat/sharp pitch points collide
  </text>
  <text class="cap" x="-88" y="-103.5">
    Rendered-view elevation, 1:1 mm.  Side installed in the neck plywood.
  </text>
  <text class="cap" x="-88" y="-99.5">
    Shared flange hosts two independent clicky stacks.  Shafts converge
    inside the flange from 16 mm barrel spacing down to 3.4 mm at the paddle tips.
  </text>

  <!-- ======================================================================
       CENTRE LINES (one per stack)
         Left stack axis at x = -8 (nat),  right stack axis at x = +8 (sharp)
       ====================================================================== -->
  <line class="cl" x1="-8" y1="-95" x2="-8" y2="8"/>
  <line class="cl" x1=" 8" y1="-95" x2=" 8" y2="8"/>

  <!-- ======================================================================
       LEFT (NAT) CLICKY STACK
       ====================================================================== -->

  <!-- GREEN PUSHER (top).  OD 10 mm, H 20 mm proud + 12 mm into barrel -->
  <rect x="-13" y="-88" width="10" height="20"
        fill="url(#gradGreen)" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.6"
        rx="0.6" ry="0.6"/>
  <ellipse cx="-8" cy="-88" rx="5" ry="1.3"
           fill="#7ed080" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.5"/>
  <ellipse cx="-8" cy="-68" rx="5" ry="1.3"
           fill="#4CAF50" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.4" opacity="0.85"/>
  <!-- Pusher segment inside barrel -->
  <rect x="-13" y="-68" width="10" height="12"
        fill="#4CAF50" fill-opacity="0.9"
        stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.35"/>

  <!-- PINK CAM SLEEVE (crenellated) -->
  <path d="M -12.2,-55
           L -11.2,-58 L -10.2,-55
           L -9.2,-58  L -8.2,-55
           L -7.2,-58  L -6.2,-55
           L -5.2,-58  L -4.2,-55
           L -3.8,-55  L -3.8,-35 L -12.2,-35 Z"
        fill="url(#gradPink)" stroke="#333" stroke-width="0.45"/>

<!--
  tuner_side.svg - Clements 47 guitar-tuner, side elevation (product view).
  1:1 scale, millimeters.  Adapted from Thingiverse 6099101 by noamtsvi
  (CC-BY-NC).  Goal of this drawing: visually recognizable silhouettes of
  the real printed parts (teardrop worm-driver head + threaded shaft,
  rounded-rectangle case, thin cap, through gear post), in situ between
  two 6 mm plywood sides separated by a 12.7 mm string gap.
-->
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     width="180mm" height="160mm"
     viewBox="-80 -60 180 160">

  <defs>
    <style>
      .outl     { stroke:#333; stroke-width:0.6;  fill:none; }

      .tuner    { fill:#3a6fd4; fill-opacity:0.55; stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.6; }
      .tuner2   { fill:#3a6fd4; fill-opacity:0.40; stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.5; }
      .cap      { fill:#5a8bdc; fill-opacity:0.55; stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.5; }
      .thread   { stroke:#1d3f82; stroke-width:0.5; fill:none; stroke-linecap:round; }
      .cross    { fill:#1d3f82; stroke:none; }
      .screw    { fill:#eaeaea; stroke:#555; stroke-width:0.25; }
      .strng    { fill:#c00000; stroke:#8a0000; stroke-width:0.25; }
      .cl       { stroke:#9090b0; stroke-width:0.2; fill:none;
                  stroke-dasharray:3,1,0.4,1; }

      .lbl      { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:3.0px; fill:#111;
                  font-weight:600; }
      .lblsm    { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.4px; fill:#333; }
      .note     { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.2px; fill:#555; }
      .attr     { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:2.6px; fill:#555;
                  font-style:italic; }
      .title    { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:4.0px; fill:#111;
                  font-weight:700; }
      .callout  { stroke:#555; stroke-width:0.25; fill:none; }
    </style>
  </defs>

  <!-- ================================================================
       Attribution caption (top-left) + title
       ================================================================ -->
  <text class="attr"  x="-78" y="-55">Guitar tuner (Thingiverse 6099101 noamtsvi, CC-BY-NC)</text>
  <text class="title" x="-78" y="-50">Clements 47 - guitar tuner, side view (1:1 mm)</text>

  <!-- ================================================================
       Coordinate frame
         x = 0  : outer face of the north plywood (where the case sits)
         x grows to the right (into the string gap and beyond)
         y grows down (along the string direction, as in clicky_side.svg)
         north plywood : x = 0  .. 6
         string gap    : x = 6  .. 18.7   (12.7 mm)
         south plywood : x = 18.7 .. 24.7 (6 mm)
         case lives on the -x side of x=0.
         worm axis is VERTICAL (y), offset to the -x side of the gear post.
       ================================================================ -->

  <!-- PLYWOOD (background) -->
  <!-- North plywood, with a 16 mm clearance hole (y = -8 .. 8) for the post -->
  <path class="ply"
        d="M 0,-42 L 6,-42 L 6,-8 L 0,-8 Z"/>
  <path class="ply"
        d="M 0, 8 L 6, 8 L 6, 42 L 0, 42 Z"/>
  <text class="lblsm" x="2" y="-34" transform="rotate(-90 2 -34)">plywood (north, 6 mm)</text>

  <!-- South plywood (across the 12.7 mm string gap) -->
  <path class="ply"
        d="M 18.7,-42 L 24.7,-42 L 24.7,42 L 18.7,42 Z"/>
  <text class="lblsm" x="20.7" y="-34" transform="rotate(-90 20.7 -34)">plywood (south, 6 mm)</text>

  <!-- Gap dim hint -->
  <text class="note" x="7" y="40">12.7 mm gap</text>

  <!-- GEAR POST (through plywood, into gap) -->
  <!--
    Post is Ø 15.4 x 42.6 mm long, axis horizontal (runs along +x through
    the north ply and into the gap).  Seen edge-on in this view it reads
    as a horizontal capsule.  Starts inside the case near x = -17, ends
    inside the gap near x = +23.
  -->
  <rect class="tuner" x="-17.4" y="-7.7" width="42.6" height="15.4" rx="1.2" ry="1.2"/>
  <!-- little bevel ring where it meets the plywood (visual only) -->
  <line class="outlthin" x1="0"  y1="-7.7" x2="0"  y2="7.7"/>
  <line class="outlthin" x1="6"  y1="-7.7" x2="6"  y2="7.7"/>
  <line class="cl" x1="-25" y1="0" x2="30" y2="0"/>

  <!-- String wrapping around the post inside the gap.  Small red circle
       tangent to the post on the gap side. -->
  <circle class="strng" cx="12.4" cy="7.7" r="0.55"/>
  <text class="lblsm" x="14.2" y="9.4">string</text>

  <!-- ================================================================
       CASE - rounded rectangle on the outer face of the north plywood.
       Footprint in this view: 39.2 (y, along string) x 19.4 (x, out).
       We centre it so the worm axis (x = -9.7) lies near the middle.
       Case x: -19.4 .. 0.  Case y: -10 .. 29.2.
       ================================================================ -->
  <rect class="tuner" x="-19.4" y="-10" width="19.4" height="39.2" rx="2.8" ry="2.8"/>

  <!-- Cutout hint where the worm enters the case (small arc on the bottom,
       where the knob protrudes).  The worm exits the case on the -y side,
       i.e. at the top.  But the real tuner has the knob hanging out the
       far end of the case (along the worm axis).  We show a small
       rectangular cutout on the -x face where the shaft / cap meet. -->
  <rect x="-19.4" y="-3" width="0.5" height="23.7" fill="#1d3f82" fill-opacity="0.8" stroke="none"/>

  <!-- Small arch on the inner (+x) face where the gear post exits: -->
  <path class="outlthin"
        d="M 0,-8 A 2 2 0 0 1 0,8"/>

  <!-- ================================================================
       CAP - thin retaining plate on the outer (-x) face of the case.
       Footprint: 23.7 (y) x 4.3 (x).  Two small screws.
       ================================================================ -->
  <rect class="cap" x="-23.7" y="-3" width="4.3" height="23.7" rx="0.8" ry="0.8"/>

  <path class="outlthin" d="M -22.2,18.2 L -20.9,18.2 M -21.55,17.55 L -21.55,18.85"/>

  <!-- ================================================================
       WORM DRIVER - the teardrop-headed part from guitartuner.png.
       Oriented with the axis vertical (along +y), head at the top,
       threaded shaft below the head.
       We draw the ACTUAL PART OUTSIDE the case (to the south / below the

       Teardrop head: ~19 wide x 25 tall.  Neck: short transition, ~6 mm.
       Threaded shaft: ~10 wide x 25 tall, 4-5 thread coils.
       Total length ~47.7 mm.
       ================================================================ -->

    <!-- Teardrop head.
         Constructed as two cubic curves joining:

         OD ~10 mm, axis vertical, from y=31.5 to y=47.7 (16 mm visible).

<script src="svg-pan-zoom.min.js"></script>
<script>
  const panzoomInstances = {};

  async function loadView(el) {
    const src = el.dataset.src;
    try {
      const resp = await fetch(src);
      if (!resp.ok) throw new Error(resp.status + ' ' + resp.statusText);
      let text = await resp.text();
      // strip XML prolog for safe inline injection
      text = text.replace(/<\?xml[^?]*\?>/, '');
      el.innerHTML = text;
      const svg = el.querySelector('svg');
      if (!svg) return;
      // ensure it fills the container
      svg.removeAttribute('width');
      svg.removeAttribute('height');
      svg.setAttribute('width', '100%');
      svg.setAttribute('height', '100%');

      // Tighten the viewBox using svg.getBBox() on the root, which includes
      // child transforms. We hide the background <rect> while measuring so
      // it doesn't dilate the bbox.
      try {
        const bgRects = Array.from(svg.querySelectorAll('rect')).filter(
          r => (r.getAttribute('fill') || '').toLowerCase() === '#fff' &&
               r.parentNode === svg);
        bgRects.forEach(r => r.setAttribute('visibility', 'hidden'));
        const bb = svg.getBBox();
        bgRects.forEach(r => r.removeAttribute('visibility'));
        if (bb && bb.width > 0 && bb.height > 0) {
          const pad = Math.max(bb.width, bb.height) * 0.03;
          svg.setAttribute('viewBox',
            (bb.x - pad) + ' ' + (bb.y - pad) + ' ' +
            (bb.width + 2*pad) + ' ' + (bb.height + 2*pad));
        }
      } catch (e) { /* fall back to the file's viewBox */ }

      const pz = svgPanZoom(svg, {
        zoomEnabled: true,
        controlIconsEnabled: false,
        fit: true,
        center: true,
        minZoom: 0.2,
        maxZoom: 40,
        zoomScaleSensitivity: 0.3,
      });
      panzoomInstances[el.id] = pz;
    } catch (e) {
      const isFile = location.protocol === 'file:';
      const hint = isFile
        ? 'You opened this file directly (<code>file://</code>); browsers block fetch from disk. '
          + 'Run <code>python3 -m http.server 8001</code> from the project folder '
          + 'and open <code>http://localhost:8001/</code> instead.'
        : 'Served from <code>' + location.origin + '</code> but <code>' + src + '</code> was not found. '
          + 'Make sure the server is running in the <strong>Erand</strong> folder (on port 8001). '
          + 'Check: <code>curl -I ' + location.origin + '/' + src + '</code>.';
      el.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:1em;color:#a00;font-size:13px">'
        + 'Failed to load <code>' + src + '</code>: ' + e.message + '<br>' + hint + '</p>';
    }
  }

  document.querySelectorAll('.view-box').forEach(loadView);

  document.querySelectorAll('.reset-btn').forEach(btn => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const pz = panzoomInstances[btn.dataset.target];
      if (pz) { pz.resetZoom(); pz.center(); pz.fit(); }
    });
  });

  // Expand/collapse panel to fill the viewport.
  function toggleFullscreen(panel) {
    const entering = !panel.classList.contains('fullscreen');
    panel.classList.toggle('fullscreen');
    document.body.classList.toggle('has-fullscreen', entering);
    // Swap the button label between "expand" and "back".
    const btn = panel.querySelector('.expand-btn');
    if (btn) btn.textContent = entering ? 'back' : 'expand';
    // Re-fit the pan-zoom instance so the SVG rescales to the new panel size.
    const viewBox = panel.querySelector('.view-box');
    if (viewBox) {
      const pz = panzoomInstances[viewBox.id];
      if (pz) setTimeout(() => { pz.resize(); pz.fit(); pz.center(); }, 30);
    }
  }
  document.querySelectorAll('.expand-btn').forEach(btn => {
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const panel = btn.closest('.panel');
      if (panel) toggleFullscreen(panel);
    });
  });
  // Escape key exits fullscreen.
  document.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
    if (e.key === 'Escape') {
      const fs = document.querySelector('.panel.fullscreen');
      if (fs) toggleFullscreen(fs);
    }
  });
</script>

"""inkscape_frame.py — single source of truth for the Inkscape → authoring
frame offset used by the v2 neck pipeline.

Per HANDOFF.md, the v2 SVG (`erand47jc_v2.svg` and its optimized descendant
`erand47jc_v2_opt.svg`) is authored in an Inkscape coordinate frame whose
origin is shifted from the project-wide authoring frame by `(INKSCAPE_DX,
INKSCAPE_DY)`. To convert an Inkscape-frame coordinate to the authoring
frame, ADD `(INKSCAPE_DX, INKSCAPE_DY)`; to convert the other direction,
subtract them.

Before v2 this offset was (+51.9, +121.64). During the v2 viewBox update
the user shifted everything by +40.37 mm in Inkscape y, giving the current
value (+51.9, +81.27). Any downstream code that needs to cross between the
frames should import from here rather than redefining the constants.
"""

INKSCAPE_DX = 51.9
INKSCAPE_DY = 81.27    # post-viewBox-shift translation (v2 frame)


def to_authoring(pt):
    """Inkscape-frame (x, y) -> authoring-frame (x, y)."""
    return (pt[0] + INKSCAPE_DX, pt[1] + INKSCAPE_DY)


def to_inkscape(pt):
    """Authoring-frame (x, y) -> Inkscape-frame (x, y)."""
    return (pt[0] - INKSCAPE_DX, pt[1] - INKSCAPE_DY)

<<<FILE|5a5120a9f15d957c|utf8|bGVnMl9iZXppZXIucHk>>>
"""
leg2_bezier.py
==============

Replace Leg 2 of the neck outline with a smooth Bezier fit through the
geodesic polyline, keeping Leg 1 (scalloped geodesic) and Leg 3 (straight
column) unchanged.

Strategy:
  1. Run neck_geodesic to get the feasible polyline for Leg 2 (ST -> NT).
  2. Densify the polyline (tesselate arcs).
  3. Fit cubic Beziers through it using Schneider's bezierfit.
  4. Force tangents at ST (soundboard slope) and NT (vertical down the column).
  5. Verify no sample on any fitted Bezier enters a buffer circle; if any
     do, increase max_error tolerance so Schneider subdivides more.
  6. Emit SVG: Leg 1 as geodesic arcs+lines, Leg 2 as Bezier curves, Leg 3
     as straight line.
"""

import math
import os
import re
import sys

import numpy as np

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
import build_harp as bh
import neck_geodesic as ng
import bezierfit

R = bh.R_BUFFER
NB = np.array(bh.NB, dtype=float)
NT = np.array(bh.NT, dtype=float)
ST = np.array(bh.ST, dtype=float)
SOUNDBOARD_DIR = np.array(bh._SOUNDBOARD_DIR, dtype=float)


def densify(segments, step=2.0, side='north'):
    """Tesselate a geodesic segments list into a dense polyline.
    side='south' => outer = larger y.  side='north' => outer = smaller y."""
    pts = []
    for kind, data in segments:
        if kind == 'line':
            p1, p2 = data
            if not pts: pts.append(tuple(p1))
            pts.append(tuple(p2))
        else:
            C, r, p_s, p_e = data
            a_s = math.atan2(p_s[1] - C[1], p_s[0] - C[0])
            a_e = math.atan2(p_e[1] - C[1], p_e[0] - C[0])
            ccw = (a_e - a_s) % (2 * math.pi)
            cw = (a_s - a_e) % (2 * math.pi)
            mid_ccw_y = C[1] + r * math.sin(a_s + ccw / 2)
            mid_cw_y = C[1] + r * math.sin(a_s - cw / 2)
            if side == 'south':
                delta = ccw if mid_ccw_y > mid_cw_y else -cw
            else:
                delta = ccw if mid_ccw_y < mid_cw_y else -cw
            n_arc = max(2, int(abs(delta) * r / step))
            for k in range(1, n_arc + 1):
                a = a_s + delta * k / n_arc
                pts.append((C[0] + r * math.cos(a), C[1] + r * math.sin(a)))
    out = [pts[0]]
    for p in pts[1:]:
        if math.hypot(p[0] - out[-1][0], p[1] - out[-1][1]) > 0.1:
            out.append(p)
    return np.array(out)


def bezier_samples(bez, n=40):
    ts = np.linspace(0, 1, n)
    out = np.zeros((n, 2))
    for k, t in enumerate(ts):
        mt = 1 - t
        out[k] = (mt**3 * bez[0] + 3 * mt * mt * t * bez[1]
                  + 3 * mt * t * t * bez[2] + t**3 * bez[3])
    return out


def _all_buffers():
    strings = bh.build_strings()
    all_buf = []
    for s in strings:
        if s['has_flat_buffer']: all_buf.append(s['flat_buffer'])
        if s['has_sharp_buffer']: all_buf.append(s['sharp'])
    return np.array(all_buf)


def _fit_leg(start, end, circles, side, left_tan, right_tan, label):
    all_buf = _all_buffers()
    segs = ng.geodesic_outline(start, end, circles, side=side)
    poly = densify(segs, step=1.5, side=side)
    OFFSET = 0.6
    off = np.zeros_like(poly)
    for i, P in enumerate(poly):
        if i == 0: tan = poly[1] - poly[0]
        elif i == len(poly)-1: tan = poly[-1] - poly[-2]
        else: tan = poly[i+1] - poly[i-1]
        tan = tan / max(np.linalg.norm(tan), 1e-9)
        nx = -tan[1]; ny = tan[0]
        if (side == 'north' and ny > 0) or (side == 'south' and ny < 0):
            nx, ny = -nx, -ny
        off[i] = P + OFFSET * np.array([nx, ny])
    poly = off
    poly[0] = start; poly[-1] = end
    print(f"  {label} polyline: {len(poly)} points")

    # If left_tan is None, use natural direction from polyline
    if left_tan is None:
        v = poly[1] - poly[0]; left_tan = v / np.linalg.norm(v)
    if right_tan is None:
        v = poly[-2] - poly[-1]; right_tan = v / np.linalg.norm(v)

    for max_err in (6.0, 4.0, 2.5, 1.5, 0.8, 0.4):
        beziers = bezierfit._fit_cubic(poly, left_tan, right_tan, max_err)
        violated = False; min_d = np.inf
        for b in beziers:
            pts = bezier_samples(b, 50)
            for c in all_buf:
                d = np.min(np.linalg.norm(pts - c, axis=1))
                if d < min_d: min_d = d
                if d < R - 0.05: violated = True
        print(f"    max_err={max_err}: {len(beziers)} segs, min dist {min_d:.2f}, "
              f"violation={'YES' if violated else 'no'}")
        if not violated:
            return beziers
    return beziers


def fit_leg1():
    """Leg 1 = [Bezier NB->C1sbs] + [Bezier chain C1sbs->ST].

    Symmetric with leg 2. NB->C1sbs is a single hand-built cubic with
    horizontal handles:
      - Tangent at NB         = east (+x), handle length = L_nb = 2 * L_c1
      - Tangent at C1sbs      = east (+x), handle length = L_c1
    C1sbs = south pole of the bass-most sharp buffer (the one whose south
    pole sits on y = NB[1], so the tangent line from NB is horizontal).
    Both anchors are at y = NB[1], so the segment is a straight horizontal
    line; the handles just set the Bezier's parameterization.

    After C1sbs the rest of Leg 1 is a Schneider fit through the remaining
    sharp buffers, with left_tangent locked to +x (continuing horizontally).
    """
    strings = bh.build_strings()
    sharps_all = [s['sharp'] for s in strings if s['has_sharp_buffer']]
    # First (bass-most) sharp buffer — its south pole defines NB's y.
    first_sharp = np.array(sharps_all[0], dtype=float)
    C1sbs = np.array([first_sharp[0], NB[1]], dtype=float)

    L_c1 = 40.0               # C1sbs entry handle
    L_nb = 2.0 * L_c1         # NB exit handle (mirrors L_st = 2*L_g7)
    east = np.array([1.0, 0.0])
    P0 = np.array(NB, dtype=float)
    P1 = P0 + L_nb * east
    P2 = C1sbs - L_c1 * east  # incoming tangent at C1sbs = +east
    P3 = C1sbs
    seg_nb_c1 = np.array([P0, P1, P2, P3])

    # Rest of leg 1: C1sbs -> ST, excluding the first sharp from the chain
    # (its tangency is already handled by the hand-built segment above).
    sharps_rest = sharps_all[1:]
    beziers_rest = _fit_leg(C1sbs, ST, sharps_rest, 'south',
                            east, None, "Leg 1 (C1sbs->ST)")
    return [seg_nb_c1] + list(beziers_rest)


def fit_leg2():
    """Leg 2 = [Bezier ST->G7fb11] + [Bezier chain G7fb11->NT].
    ST->G7fb11 segment is a single cubic Bezier with hand-set handles:
      - Tangent at ST  = SOUNDBOARD_DIR, handle length = 2*L (the ST exit handle)
      - Tangent at G7fb11 = SOUNDBOARD_DIR, handle length = L (the G7fb11 inbound)
    G7fb11 is the point on the G7 flat buffer where tangent to the circle
    is parallel to the soundboard slope, on the upper-left (~11 o'clock) side.
    Then the rest of leg 2 is a Schneider fit from G7fb11 to NT with the
    left_tangent locked to SOUNDBOARD_DIR."""
    strings = bh.build_strings()
    flats = [s['flat_buffer'] for s in reversed(strings) if s['has_flat_buffer']]
    all_buf = []
    for s in strings:
        if s['has_flat_buffer']: all_buf.append(s['flat_buffer'])
        if s['has_sharp_buffer']: all_buf.append(s['sharp'])
    all_buf = np.array(all_buf)

    # G7fb 11-o'clock: point on G7 flat buffer where circle tangent == soundboard_dir
    # (radius direction perpendicular to soundboard_dir, on upper-left side).
    G7fb = np.array(strings[-1]['flat_buffer'], dtype=float)
    sb = SOUNDBOARD_DIR
    # Two perpendicular radius options: (-sb.y, sb.x) and (sb.y, -sb.x). Pick
    # the one pointing toward upper-left of center (both components negative
    # in SVG y-down means up-left).
    r_opt1 = np.array([-sb[1], sb[0]])
    r_opt2 = np.array([ sb[1], -sb[0]])
    r_dir = r_opt1 if (r_opt1[0] < 0 and r_opt1[1] < 0) else r_opt2
    G7fb11 = G7fb + R * r_dir

    # --- First segment: ST -> G7fb11 with forced handles ---
    # ST exit handle = +sb (UP-RIGHT, along soundboard extension past ST).
    # G7fb10 handle = +sb from G7fb11 so tangent at G7fb11 = -sb, matching the
    # circle's natural tangent at the 10-o'clock position when traversed
    # from ST-side to NT-side on the north face of the circle.
    L_g7 = 40.0            # G7fb entry handle (reverted to the "better" length)
    L_st = 2.0 * L_g7      # ST exit handle = 2 * G7fb entry = 80
    P0 = ST
    P1 = ST + L_st * sb          # UP-RIGHT from ST
    P2 = G7fb11 + L_g7 * sb      # UP-RIGHT from G7fb11 (tangent at G7fb11 = -sb)
    P3 = G7fb11
    seg_st_g7 = np.array([P0, P1, P2, P3])

    # --- Rest of leg 2: G7fb11 -> NT using Schneider ---
    segs = ng.geodesic_outline(G7fb11, NT, flats[1:], side='north')   # skip G7fb itself
    poly = densify(segs, step=1.5, side='north')
    # Offset the polyline 0.6 mm outward (north = smaller SVG y) along the
    # local normal so Bezier corner-cutting has headroom and won't graze
    # into any buffer circle.
    OFFSET = 0.6
    off = np.zeros_like(poly)
    for i, P in enumerate(poly):
        if i == 0:
            tan = poly[1] - poly[0]
        elif i == len(poly) - 1:
            tan = poly[-1] - poly[-2]
        else:
            tan = poly[i + 1] - poly[i - 1]
        tan = tan / max(np.linalg.norm(tan), 1e-9)
        # Outer normal on the north side: rotate tangent -90 deg (cross with +z)
        # In SVG y-down, (tx, ty) -> (ty, -tx) is right-hand turn.  North is
        # smaller y, i.e., negative y offset from the tangent's perpendicular.
        # Walking ST->NT is LEFT in x (tan has -x component); right-hand
        # perp of (-1, 0) is (0, 1) = south.  So we need the LEFT-hand perp
        # = (-ty, tx).
        nx = -tan[1]; ny = tan[0]
        # Verify it points "north" (smaller y in SVG); if not, flip.
        if ny > 0:
            nx, ny = -nx, -ny
        off[i] = P + OFFSET * np.array([nx, ny])
    poly = off
    # Preserve the true endpoints (G7fb11 and NT) exactly for the Schneider fit.
    poly[0] = G7fb11
    poly[-1] = NT
    print(f"Leg 2 (G7fb11 -> NT) polyline: {len(poly)} points (offset {OFFSET} mm outward)")

    # Tangent at G7fb11: LOCKED to -soundboard_dir (continuing the tangent from
    # the ST->G7fb11 segment in the direction of travel).
    left_tangent = -sb
    # Tangent at NT: leg 2 arrives heading DOWN the column.
    right_tangent = np.array([0.0, -1.0])

    # Try tightening tolerance until no buffer violations.
    for max_err in (6.0, 4.0, 2.5, 1.5, 0.8):
        beziers = bezierfit._fit_cubic(poly, left_tangent, right_tangent, max_err)
        violated = False
        min_d = np.inf
        for b in beziers:
            pts = bezier_samples(b, 50)
            for c in all_buf:
                d = np.min(np.linalg.norm(pts - c, axis=1))
                if d < min_d: min_d = d
                if d < R - 0.05:
                    violated = True
        print(f"  max_err={max_err}: {len(beziers)} segments, "
              f"min pin-dist={min_d:.2f} mm, violation={'YES' if violated else 'no'}")
        if not violated:
            return [seg_st_g7] + list(beziers)
    return [seg_st_g7] + list(beziers)


def beziers_to_d(beziers):
    if not beziers: return ""
    P0 = beziers[0][0]
    parts = [f"M {P0[0]:.3f} {P0[1]:.3f}"]
    for _, P1, P2, P3 in beziers:
        parts.append(f"C {P1[0]:.3f} {P1[1]:.3f} "
                     f"{P2[0]:.3f} {P2[1]:.3f} "
                     f"{P3[0]:.3f} {P3[1]:.3f}")
    return " ".join(parts)


def main():
    # Leg 1: Bezier fit (smoothed geodesic).
    print("Leg 1:")
    beziers1 = fit_leg1()
    d1 = beziers_to_d(beziers1)

    # Leg 2: Bezier fit.
    print("Leg 2:")
    beziers2 = fit_leg2()
    d2 = beziers_to_d(beziers2)

    # Leg 3: column straight.
    d3 = f"M {NT[0]:.3f} {NT[1]:.3f} L {NB[0]:.3f} {NB[1]:.3f}"

    with open(bh.OUTPUT_SVG) as f:
        content = f.read()
    content = re.sub(r'<(path|line|circle)[^>]*"#ff69b4"[^>]*/>\s*', '', content)
    pink = (
        f'<path d="{d1}" fill="none" stroke="#ff69b4" stroke-width="1.6"/>\n'
        f'<path d="{d2}" fill="none" stroke="#ff69b4" stroke-width="1.6"/>\n'
        f'<path d="{d3}" fill="none" stroke="#ff69b4" stroke-width="1.6"/>\n'
    )
    BEZ = '#1060d0'
    # Combine both legs for handle/anchor rendering.  Skip endpoint-tangent
    # handles at each leg's endpoints (those are tangent artifacts).
    def _emit_handles(beziers, show_first_P1=False, show_last_P2=False):
        out = ""
        for i, seg in enumerate(beziers):
            P0, P1, P2, P3 = seg
            show_P1 = (i != 0) or show_first_P1
            show_P2 = (i != len(beziers) - 1) or show_last_P2
            if show_P1:
                out += (
                    f'<line x1="{P0[0]:.3f}" y1="{P0[1]:.3f}" '
                    f'x2="{P1[0]:.3f}" y2="{P1[1]:.3f}" '
                    f'stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2,1.5"/>\n'
                    f'<circle cx="{P1[0]:.3f}" cy="{P1[1]:.3f}" r="1.6" '
                    f'fill="#fff" stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.6"/>\n'
                )
            if show_P2:
                out += (
                    f'<line x1="{P3[0]:.3f}" y1="{P3[1]:.3f}" '
                    f'x2="{P2[0]:.3f}" y2="{P2[1]:.3f}" '
                    f'stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2,1.5"/>\n'
                    f'<circle cx="{P2[0]:.3f}" cy="{P2[1]:.3f}" r="1.6" '
                    f'fill="#fff" stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.6"/>\n'
                )
        return out
    pink += _emit_handles(beziers1, show_first_P1=True)              # show NB exit handle
    pink += _emit_handles(beziers2, show_first_P1=True, show_last_P2=True)  # show ST exit + NT entry handles
    # Stale-loop trigger block removed:
    for i, seg in enumerate([]):
        P0, P1, P2, P3 = seg
        show_P1 = (i != 0)
        show_P2 = (i != len(beziers2) - 1)
        if show_P1:
            pink += (
                f'<line x1="{P0[0]:.3f}" y1="{P0[1]:.3f}" '
                f'x2="{P1[0]:.3f}" y2="{P1[1]:.3f}" '
                f'stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2,1.5"/>\n'
                f'<circle cx="{P1[0]:.3f}" cy="{P1[1]:.3f}" r="1.6" '
                f'fill="#fff" stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.6"/>\n'
            )
        if show_P2:
            pink += (
                f'<line x1="{P3[0]:.3f}" y1="{P3[1]:.3f}" '
                f'x2="{P2[0]:.3f}" y2="{P2[1]:.3f}" '
                f'stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="2,1.5"/>\n'
                f'<circle cx="{P2[0]:.3f}" cy="{P2[1]:.3f}" r="1.6" '
                f'fill="#fff" stroke="{BEZ}" stroke-width="0.6"/>\n'
            )
    # Anchors (endpoints of each segment, both legs, dedup by position)
    anchor_pts = []
    for seg in list(beziers1) + list(beziers2):
        for pt in (seg[0], seg[3]):
            if not anchor_pts or math.hypot(pt[0]-anchor_pts[-1][0],
                                            pt[1]-anchor_pts[-1][1]) > 0.05:
                anchor_pts.append(pt)
    for pt in anchor_pts:
        pink += (f'<circle cx="{pt[0]:.3f}" cy="{pt[1]:.3f}" r="2" fill="#000"/>\n')
    content = content.replace('</svg>', pink + '</svg>')
    with open(bh.OUTPUT_SVG, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)

    try:
        import subprocess
        subprocess.run(["rsvg-convert", "-w", str(bh.PNG_W), "-h", str(bh.PNG_H),
                        "-o", bh.OUTPUT_PNG, bh.OUTPUT_SVG], check=True)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"PNG render failed: {e}")


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

<<<FILE|5a5120a9f15d957c|utf8|bWFrZV9zdmcucHk>>>
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Render erand.dxf as an SVG with measurements in millimetres."""
import ezdxf, math

SRC = '/home/james.clements/projects/erand/erand.dxf'
OUT = '/home/james.clements/projects/erand/erand.svg'
IN_TO_MM = 25.4

NOTES = ['G','F','E','D','C','B','A']  # diatonic order descending from G7
def note_for(num):
    """String #1 = G7, descending diatonically. Returns (letter, octave)."""
    idx = num - 1
    letter = NOTES[idx % 7]
    # octave: G7 at num=1, F7=2, ..., A7? no, G is top. G7,F7,E7,D7,C7,B6,A6 ...
    # Each cycle of 7 drops one octave after C. Easier: precompute once.
    return letter

# Precompute note labels for strings 1..47
def build_notes():
    seq = []
    letters = ['G','F','E','D','C','B','A']
    octave = 7
    for i in range(47):
        letter = letters[i % 7]
        seq.append((letter, octave))
        if letter == 'A':      # next string after A is G of next-lower octave
            octave -= 1
    return seq
NOTE_TABLE = build_notes()  # index 0 -> string #1

# String diameters from ERAND.md (effective = core + 2 * wrap for wound strings)
def diameter_in(num):
    if 1  <= num <= 7 : return 0.025
    if 8  <= num <= 10: return 0.028
    if num == 11      : return 0.030
    if 12 <= num <= 14: return 0.032
    if 15 <= num <= 18: return 0.036
    if 19 <= num <= 21: return 0.040
    if 22 <= num <= 24: return 0.045
    if 25 <= num <= 27: return 0.050
    # Middle register: nylon core + nylon/steel wrap (effective = core + 2*wrap)
    if 28 <= num <= 29: return 0.045 + 2*0.008
    if 30 <= num <= 31: return 0.050 + 2*0.008
    if num == 32      : return 0.050 + 2*0.013
    if 33 <= num <= 34: return 0.055 + 2*0.013
    if num == 35      : return 0.060 + 2*0.013
    if num == 36      : return 0.060 + 2*0.016  # interpolated 0.013->0.022
    if num == 37      : return 0.060 + 2*0.019
    if num == 38      : return 0.060 + 2*0.022
    # Lower register: steel core + bronze wrap
    if num == 39      : return 0.020 + 2*0.008
    if num == 40      : return 0.020 + 2*0.010
    if num == 41      : return 0.020 + 2*0.010
    if num == 42      : return 0.022 + 2*0.013
    if num == 43      : return 0.022 + 2*0.013
    if num == 44      : return 0.024 + 2*0.013
    if num == 45      : return 0.025 + 2*0.016  # interpolated 0.013->0.020
    if num == 46      : return 0.025 + 2*0.018
    if num == 47      : return 0.026 + 2*0.020
    return 0.04

def color_for(letter):
    if letter == 'C': return '#c00000'  # red
    if letter == 'F': return '#1060d0'  # blue
    return '#888'                       # gray

doc = ezdxf.readfile(SRC)
msp = doc.modelspace()

strings, on_ticks, keys, texts = [], [], [], []
for L in msp.query('LINE'):
    dx, dy = L.dxf.end.x - L.dxf.start.x, L.dxf.end.y - L.dxf.start.y
    length = math.hypot(dx, dy)
    ang = math.degrees(math.atan2(dy, dx))
    if abs(length - 0.25) < 1e-3 and abs(ang) < 1:
        on_ticks.append(L)
    elif abs(length - 1.53) < 0.02 and abs(ang - 78) < 2:
        keys.append(L)
    elif abs(ang - 90) < 1 and length > 2:
        strings.append(L)
for T in msp.query('TEXT'):
    texts.append(T)

# Rank strings by active length
srows = []
for L in strings:
    y0, y1 = sorted([L.dxf.start.y, L.dxf.end.y])
    srows.append({'x': L.dxf.start.x, 'yg': y0, 'yf': y1,
                  'len': y1 - y0, 'ticks': [], 'key_tip': None})
for i, r in enumerate(sorted(srows, key=lambda r: r['len'])):
    r['num'] = i + 1
srows_by_x = sorted(srows, key=lambda r: r['x'])

for T in on_ticks:
    cx = T.dxf.start.x + 0.125
    cy = T.dxf.start.y
    best = min(srows, key=lambda r: abs(r['x'] - cx))
    if abs(best['x'] - cx) < 0.15:
        best['ticks'].append(cy)
    else:
        best2 = min(srows, key=lambda r: abs(r['x'] + 0.319 - cx) + abs(r['yf'] + 1.497 - cy))
        best2['key_tip'] = (cx, cy)

# Column geometry (from photo measurement + DXF-mm calibration at 0.1616 mm/px):
#   column shaft width:      39 mm
#   column -> bass string:   53 mm (at mid-shaft; 57 at top, 50 at bottom)
#   column shaft length:   1467 mm
#   column tilt from vert: +0.49 deg  (top leans away from strings)
COL_W_MM         = 39.0
COL_TO_BASS_MM   = 50.0     # 50 mm gap from column inner face to bass string
# Column is PARALLEL to the strings (both vertical in DXF), running floor -> top of harp.
BASS_X_IN   = min(r['x'] for r in srows)                    # = 10.0 in (bass string)
BASS_X_MM   = BASS_X_IN * IN_TO_MM                          # 254.0 mm
COL_RIGHT_MM = BASS_X_MM - COL_TO_BASS_MM                   # 204.0 mm (inner / string-facing face)
COL_LEFT_MM  = COL_RIGHT_MM - COL_W_MM                      # 165.0 mm (outer face)
BASS_YF_IN   = next(r['yf'] for r in srows if abs(r['x']-BASS_X_IN) < 1e-6)
# Column extends floor -> top of harp:
#   floor (bottom) = y below the lowest grommet (base sits below strings)
#   top            = y above the highest feature (above all key-tips / tuning pins)
all_y_mm = (
    [r['yg'] * IN_TO_MM for r in srows]
  + [r['yf'] * IN_TO_MM for r in srows]
  + [r['key_tip'][1] * IN_TO_MM for r in srows if r.get('key_tip')]
)
COL_TOP_MM_Y = max(all_y_mm) + 60                           # a bit above the highest feature
COL_BOT_MM_Y = 0.0                                          # floor
COL_LENGTH_MM = COL_TOP_MM_Y - COL_BOT_MM_Y
# Convert back to inches for the global view transform
COL_LEFT_IN  = COL_LEFT_MM  / IN_TO_MM
COL_RIGHT_IN = COL_RIGHT_MM / IN_TO_MM
COL_TOP_IN   = COL_TOP_MM_Y / IN_TO_MM
COL_BOT_IN   = COL_BOT_MM_Y / IN_TO_MM
